series: Los Angeles Lakers vs Houston Rockets
source_context: User-provided ESPN box scores, team stats, PBP Stats rebound tables, player tracking rebound data, team box-outs, on/off context, and market screenshots from LAL-HOU Games 1-2 with Game 3 prep.

rebound_environment.read: Houston created extreme offensive rebound pressure despite Lakers box-out edge. LAL efficiency suppressed its own rebound volume.

player_rebound_notes.Deandre Ayton: 11 rebounds on 19 chances; strong Game 1 but later exposed by minutes volatility.
player_rebound_notes.LeBron James: 8 rebounds on 13 chances; stable defensive rebound profile.
12 rebounds on 14 chances; strong result but conversion-regression risk.
player_rebound_notes.Tari Eason: 10 rebounds on 13 chances; elite per-minute activity.
player_rebound_notes.Alperen Sengun: 8 rebounds on 16 chances; stable chance base with room for rebound growth.

rebound_environment.read: Total rebound pool repeated exactly at 79. Houston offensive glass persisted despite zero recorded team box-outs.

player_rebound_notes.Alperen Sengun: 11 rebounds on 22 chances; strongest Game 2 chance profile.
player_rebound_notes.Tari Eason: 8 rebounds on 10 chances; repeatable activity edge confirmed.
player_rebound_notes.Deandre Ayton: 5 rebounds on 15 chances; minutes dropped from 35.1 to 26.6, triggering center displacement risk.
player_rebound_notes.Kevin Durant: 6 rebounds on 10 chances; low-to-mid chance base but clean conversion.
6 rebounds on 10 chances; Game 1 spike regressed.

offensive_glass.takeaway: Houston owns the offensive glass environment.

series_level_findings.box_out_rule: Team box-outs sharpen the read but are support variables only. Player tracking chances and PBP rebound zones carry more predictive weight.

repeatable_edges.reason: 10 and 8 rebounds across Games 1-2 with elite activity.

downgrades.reason: Minutes and conversion collapsed in Game 2.

rules: If total rebound pool repeats within +/-3 across consecutive playoff games, upgrade rebound-environment stability for Game 3.
If player rebound chances remain >=10 and adjusted chance conversion >=55% across consecutive games, permit ladder consideration.
If team offensive rebounds repeat at 15+ in consecutive playoff games, apply OREB persistence tag to active forwards/centers.
If center minutes drop by 8+ minutes between games, activate CENTER_DISPLACEMENT_RISK_ON for high rebound lines.

audit_result.backtest_support_level: Games 1-2 fully formalized from user-provided data; Game 3 projection seed attached.
